instructions:
Two false positives appeared in the iOS validator after the 0076 surface swap (more comprehensive ABI surface revealed validator-side matching gaps). Both are validator-side, not surface-side. Two small fixes:

## Fix 1: Swift stdlib + Foundation allowlist

`String.self` was flagged in edit-comment.mdx. `String` is Swift standard library, not AmitySDK. The Android validator has analogous `Environment`/`Context`/etc. allowlist for platform classes (per task 0039). Mirror that pattern for iOS.

Add a SWIFT_PLATFORM_ALLOWLIST to ios-accuracy-validator.py covering common Swift stdlib + Foundation + UIKit + SwiftUI types that may appear in iOS doc code blocks but aren't AmitySDK refs. Minimum set:

  String, Int, Double, Float, Bool, Array, Dictionary, Set, Optional,  // stdlib
  Bundle, URL, Data, Date, FileManager, NotificationCenter, UserDefaults,  // Foundation
  UIView, UIViewController, UIImage, UIColor, UIApplication,  // UIKit
  View, Text, Image, Button, NavigationView,  // SwiftUI
  Result, Error, Task, MainActor  // common Swift concurrency

When a ref's top-level type matches an allowlist entry, skip the validation (treat as valid platform-class reference). Keep the allowlist tight — only add classes that DO appear in the current drift report or are universally common.

## Fix 2: uikit/ path exclusion

`Bundle.main.path` was flagged from `uikit/customization/component-styling.mdx` — that's the UIKit SDK docs path, not the core iOS SDK. The Flutter validator has `EXCLUDED_PREFIXES` covering `uikit/` and `social-plus-sdk/video-new/` per task 0034 — mirror that for iOS.

Add EXCLUDED_PREFIXES to ios-accuracy-validator.py:
  - `uikit/` (Amity UIKit iOS SDK docs — separate from core)
  - `social-plus-sdk/video-new/` (Live Streaming SDK)

When a page path starts with one of these prefixes, skip it during scan.

## Verification

After both fixes, re-run the iOS validator. The 2 false positives should be gone (`total_issues: 0`). Make sure no LEGITIMATE drift is newly hidden — spot-check the allowlist isn't too broad (e.g., `String.specificAmityMethod` would still be flagged because `specificAmityMethod` wouldn't be in the surface).

## Out of scope

- Swift compiler-level recognition of imported modules (too complex for v1; the allowlist is fine).
- Same allowlist for Android/Flutter (already done in those validators in earlier rounds).

## Success criteria

- iOS validator reports `total_issues: 0` after the fixes.
- The allowlist + EXCLUDED_PREFIXES are documented in the file with comments explaining why each entry is included.

Report in `notes`: the exact allowlist + EXCLUDED_PREFIXES values added; any other refs in the current drift report that LOOK like they should be allowlist candidates (so we don't ship a too-narrow allowlist that breaks on the next surface bump).

validators.name: ios_validator_clean_after_allowlist
validators.command: python3 .docs-ops/extractors/ios-docc-extractor.py > /dev/null && python3 .docs-ops/validators/ios-accuracy-validator.py > /dev/null && python3 -c "import json; d=json.load(open('.docs-ops/evals/ios-accuracy-drift.json')); s=d['stats']; print(f'iOS drift: {s.get(\"total_issues\",0)}'); assert s.get('total_issues',1) == 0, f'expected 0, got {s.get(\"total_issues\")}'"
